subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Children and Poison Risk
description: A micro-lesson that makes Children and Poison Risk usable.

hook.text: A toddler finds colorful laundry pods under the sink. To them, they look like candy.

buildup.text: Children under 5 account for nearly half of all poison control calls in the US.

discovery.text: Their smaller body mass means a smaller dose creates a bigger effect—faster.

twist.text: Child-resistant caps reduced poisoning deaths by 45%, but 'resistant' doesn't mean 'proof.'

climax.text: Storing chemicals up high, locked away, and in original containers prevents most incidents.

punchline.text: Kids explore with their mouths. Every cabinet is a potential poison cabinet.

quiz.question: What age group accounts for nearly half of US poison control calls?
